phase1.output: (Window with working form — enter name and message, submit button enables, label updates)
phase1.explanation:
FXML controllers connect UI to logic:

fx:controller attribute:
• Specifies the controller class
• Must be a full class name

@FXML annotation:
• Marks fields bound to FXML elements
• Matches fx:id in FXML
• Marks event handler methods

initialize() method:
• Called after FXML is loaded
• Set up initial state
• Add listeners

Event handlers:
• Method with @FXML annotation
• Matches onAction="#methodName" in FXML

Loading FXML:
• FXMLLoader loader = new FXMLLoader(...)
• Parent root = loader.load()
• Get controller: loader.getController()

FXML properties:
• fx:id — identifies element (matches field name)
• fx:controller — specifies controller class
• onAction="#methodName" — event handler binding

Controller lifecycle:
1. FXML loaded
2. Controller instantiated
3. @FXML fields injected
4. initialize() called
5. UI is ready

Benefits:
• Clean separation of concerns
• Easy to modify UI
• Reusable controllers

phase2.errorMessage:
Exception in thread "JavaFX Application Thread" java.lang.NullPointerException
    at WrongController.handleEvent(WrongController.java:8)
    ...
(label is null because it wasn't injected!)

phase3.prompt:
Create a controller for this FXML:

<VBox fx:controller="CalculatorController">
    <TextField fx:id="num1Field" promptText="Number 1" />
    <TextField fx:id="num2Field" promptText="Number 2" />
    <Label fx:id="resultLabel" text="Result: " />
    <HBox>
        <Button fx:id="addBtn" text="Add" onAction="#handleAdd" />
        <Button fx:id="subtractBtn" text="Subtract" onAction="#handleSubtract" />
    </HBox>
</VBox>

Write the CalculatorController class with:
1. @FXML fields for all elements
2. handleAdd() method that adds the two numbers
3. handleSubtract() method that subtracts the two numbers
4. initialize() method that sets up any initial state

Note: No need to write the full app — just the controller class.
dialogueHints: Fields: @FXML private TextField num1Field, num2Field; @FXML private Label resultLabel;
phase3.openingDialogue: FXML controllers — wire up your UI! 🔌
